page: Amazon Web Services r5dn.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server instance powered by dual Intel Xeon 8259CL processors. It features 48 physical cores and 96 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with 3600 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. It also delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps, optimized for high-throughput networking and Elastic Block Store performance.

Benchmark data highlights strong multi-core and memory subsystem performance. The server achieves a strong rating in multi-core CPU stress tests and Passmark CPU scores, aided by hardware-accelerated instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AES cryptography. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, reaching 4542.25 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 224.19 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Database and key-value store performance is also notable, with Redis SET operations reaching 76.05 million operations per second and Passmark database operations scoring in the top tier.

While multi-threaded execution and memory bandwidth are strong, single-core performance remains in the average tier, as shown by stress-ng and Geekbench single-core metrics. Additionally, without dedicated GPU hardware, the instance exhibits weak to poor performance in LLM text generation tasks, making it unsuitable for real-time generative AI inference. Given its high memory density, fast local NVMe storage, and 100 Gbps networking, this instance is highly cost-efficient for large-scale in-memory databases, high-performance computing workloads, data-intensive ETL pipelines, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services r5dn.metal is a memory-optimized bare-metal server configured with 96 vCPUs (48 physical cores) via Intel Xeon 8259CL processors running at 3.1 GHz. It features 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory, 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. Benchmark results demonstrate strong multi-core processing capabilities, high memory bandwidth, and excellent database performance, highlighted by 76.05 million Redis SET operations per second. However, single-core performance is average, and the lack of a GPU results in poor LLM text generation speeds. This instance is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale databases, and high-throughput network applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5dn.metal is a memory-optimized bare-metal server featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, 3600 GB NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for high-throughput database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5dn.metal is a bare-metal server with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, 3600 GB NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-bandwidth, bare-metal server with massive memory and local NVMe storage optimized for demanding database and data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 768 GB DDR4 RAM for memory-intensive applications
3600 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with EBS optimization
Strong multi-core CPU and high-throughput memory bandwidth performance
Best suited for in-memory databases, ETL pipelines, and high-throughput web serving
